characters.name: Alex Montez
characters.description: Alex MontezBiographical InformationFull NameAlex MontezGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyJuan Montez(Uncle)Maria Montez(Aunt)Unnamed(Grandmother)Yolanda Montez(Cousin)[1]Character InformationOccupationStudentSeriesFirst Appearance"Wildcat"Last Appearance"Wildcat"PortrayalJonathan BlancoAlex Montezis a minor character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. Alex is the nephew ofJuan MontezandMaria Montez, and the cousin ofYolanda Montez. Alex Montez was born and raised inBlue Valley, Nebraska, by a Catholic family. His was raised by his auntMariaand his uncleJuan. He also lived with his cousinYolandaand agrandmother. He attended Blue Valley schools and frequently went to church with his family.

characters.name: Artemis Crock
characters.description: Artemis CrockArtemisCostumedBiographical InformationFull NameArtemis CrockBorn2005GenderFemaleAlter EgoArtemis(Current)Sportsmaster 2.0(Injustice Unlimited; formerly)Nickname(s)Artie(by Larry)StatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyLarry Crock†(Father)Paula Brooks†(Mother)FriendsCindy BurmanIsaac Bowin†Beth ChapelCourtney WhitmoreYolanda MontezBarbara WhitmoreMike DuganEnemiesEclipsoJordan Mahkent†Ultra-Humanite†Shiro ItoSofus MahkentCharacter InformationOccupationStudentEducationBlue Valley High SchoolNebraska State UniversityAffiliationJustice Society of AmericaInjustice Unlimited(Formerly)SeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalStella Smith Artemis Crockis a recurring character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. She is a star-athlete atBlue Valley High School, paving a name for herself as the first female quarterback at the school. She excels in sports under the guidance of her parents, who are secretly members of theInjustice Society of America. When she learns about her parents past, Artemis teams up withInjustice Unlimitedto get revenge on theJSAfor the part they played in her parents' arrest.

Barbara is the mother ofCourtney Whitmore, the wife ofPat Dugan, and the stepmother ofMike Dugan. With her daughter inhigh school, Barbara is diving back into the workforce but striving to balance workplace demands with the commitments of both matrimony and motherhood.

characters.name: Becky Sharpe
characters.description: Becky SharpeBiographical InformationFull NameRebecca SharpeGenderFemaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyUnnamed(Grandfather)Steve Sharpe†(Father)Unnamed(Mother)FriendsCourtney WhitmoreSeriesFirst Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalKelsey Rose HealeyRebecca "Becky" Sharpeis a minor character inThe CWseries,Stargirl. She is the estranged daughter ofSteve Sharpe, and unknowingly becomes the catalyst of his decision to try and be better. Rebecca was born toSteve Sharpe, a member of theInjustice Society of Americaand a criminal, and an unnamed mother. Her mother never told Steven about Becky, choosing to put her up for adoption when Becky was two-years-old.

characters.name: Beth Chapel
characters.description: Beth ChapelBethDr. Mid-NiteBiographical InformationFull NameBeth ChapelBornMay 15, 2004GenderFemaleAlter EgoDr. Mid-NiteNickname(s)Best Pal(ByChuck)Nerd, Chapel(ByArtemis)Dr. Mid-Nite Jr(ByCindy)Doc(ByUltra-Humanite)StatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyBridget Chapel(Mother)James Chapel(Father)RomancesRick Tyler(Fiancé)FriendsCourtney WhitmoreYolanda MontezChuckCharles McNiderRick TylerJennie-Lynn HaydenPat DuganMike DuganArtemis CrockBarbara WhitmoreCindy BurmanSofus MahkentEnemiesInjustice Society of America(Defeated)Injustice UnlimitedEclipso(Defeated)Ultra-Humanite(Defeated)Character InformationOccupationStudentEducationBlue Valley High SchoolAffiliationJustice Society of AmericaSeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalAnjelika Washington Beth Chapelis a main character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. Beth Chapel is a brilliant student atBlue Valley Highwhose intellectual nature made her an outcast amongst her peers. She forms an unlikely friendship withCourtney Whitmore,Yolanda Montez, andRick Tyleras they work together to stop the villains of the past. Aided by theartificial intelligenceof Golden Age JSA memberCharles McNider, Beth works with her friends to continue the legacy of theJustice Society of Americaas the superheroineDoctor Mid-Nite.

characters.name: Bobbie Burman
characters.description: Bobbie BurmanBiographical InformationFull NameBobbie BurmanGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyCindy Burman(Stepdaughter)RomancesDr. Ito(Fake Husband)Pat Dugan(Former Attraction)EnemiesCindy BurmanDr. ItoEclipsoSeriesFirst Appearance"S.T.R.I.P.E."Last Appearance"Summer School: Chapter Two"PortrayalLesa Wilson Bobbie Burmanwas a minor character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. She was the step-mother ofCindy Burmanand the newest wife ofDr. Ito. She served as Cindy's mother in public, only to be treated as a slave once home. She doted on Cindy's every word out of fear for Dr. Ito's retribution. Bobbie is initially portrayed as the positive, encouraging and well-involved step-mother ofCindy Burman. She attends pep rallies and open houses but is talked down to by Cindy. In reality, Bobbie is petrified of the consequences she might face at the hands of Cindy or Dragon King if she speaks out of turn or upsets them. This fear keeps her close to the family and makes her a loyal subject to them. She tries to stay positive and hide her fear but caves in when threatened by Cindy with the idea of Dragon King punishing her again. Bobbie is also shown to have a very flirtatious side, likely the result of having virtually no physical contact with anyone save for Cindy. She showed very little restraint, making her intentions clear to Pat with no hesitation, trying to convince him to sleep with her instantly which made Pat quite uncomfortable.

characters.name: Brian Tanner Balloid
characters.description: Brian Tanner BalloidBiographical InformationFull NameBrian Tanner BalloidBorn2004/5(Age 15/16)GenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFriendsHenry King Jr.†Travis ThomasEnemiesYolanda MontezCharacter InformationOccupationStudentEducationBlue Valley High SchoolSeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Five: The Thief"PortrayalJasun JabbarBrian Tanner Balloidis a minor character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. He is a student atBlue Valley High Schooland a member of the BVHS football team. He is close friends withHenry King Jr.andTravis Thomas. Henryapproaches a table in the cafeteria alongside his other jock friends, Brian, and Travis. Henry leans to the right ofYolanda, smirking at her despite her being uncomfortable with his presence. Brian and the other teen shroud to the left of her, boxing her in. Brian remains quiet but is expressive during the confrontation between Henry andCourtney. Brian leaves when Henry storms off.

characters.name: Bridget Chapel
characters.description: Bridget ChapelBiographical InformationFull NameBridget ChapelGenderFemaleAlter EgoHootieStatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyBeth Chapel(Daughter)RomancesJames Chapel(Husband)FriendsCourtney WhitmorePat DuganCharles McNiderRick TylerEnemiesEclipsoCharacter InformationOccupationCardiothoracic SurgeonEducationDoctorate DegreeAffiliationBlue Valley Medical CenterJustice Society of AmericaSeriesFirst Appearance"S.T.R.I.P.E."Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalKron MooreDr. Bridget Chapelis a recurring character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. She is the mother of the heroineBeth Chapeland a brilliant doctor atBlue Valley Medical Centerwho worked closely withHenry King Sr.After learning the truth about her daughter being a superhero, Bridget and James leap into action to help their daughter navigate a new part of her life. She becomes Dr. Mid-Nite's sidekick,Hootie, in honor of Dr.Charles McNider'strusty owl. Bridget marriedJames Chapeland had a daughter with him,Beth. According to Beth, Bridget and James sacrificed much of their lives and careers to raise their daughter and help her grow as a young woman. Bridget presumably took a step back from her duties atBlue Valley Medical Center, though she maintained a professional relationship with Dr.Henry King Sr., her colleague at BVMC. When Beth entered high school, the couple went back to work and pushed Beth to be more independent and make real friends.

characters.name: Bruce Gordon
characters.description: Bruce GordonBiographical InformationFull NameBruce GordonGenderMaleStatusDeceasedRelationshipsEnemiesEclipsoJustice Society of AmericaCharacter InformationOccupationExplorerSeriesFirst Appearance"Summer School: Chapter Nine"Last Appearance"Summer School: Chapter Nine"PortrayalJason DavisMilo Stein(Age 10)Bruce Gordonis a minor guest character inThe CWseries,Stargirl. Bruce is a traveler and explorer who rose to fame after his trip to Diablo Island, which resulted in the discovery of a lost civilization. He had secretly found a great power inside aBlack Diamond. What started as a way to earn fame and riches, led Bruce to become a host for the demonEclipso. Bruce was a struggling explorer who somehow rediscovered the lost civilization of Diablo Island in his adult years. When he arrived, everyone had been dead for decades. He found a powerful gem calledThe Black Diamondand feel victim to the evil entity within -Eclipso. Bruce brought the diamond back to the states with him and used the power of the diamond to achieved wealth and fame. He became obsessed and reliant on the power to the point where he couldn't put the diamond down.

characters.name: Buddy
characters.description: BuddyBiographical InformationFull NameBuddyGenderMaleNickname(s)Demon Dog, Traitor(byCourtney)Good Doggy(byBeth)StatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyPat Dugan(Owner)Mike Dugan(Owner)Barbara Whitmore(Co-Owner)Courtney Whitmore(Co-Owner)FriendsJustinBeth ChapelJennie-Lynn HaydenCharles McNiderUltra-Humanite†(Disguised asSylvester Pemberton)Character InformationOccupationPet dogSeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"Buddyis a recurring character inThe CWandDC UniverseseriesStargirl. Buddy is an English bulldog and the loyal pet ofMike DuganandPat Dugan. He later moves toBlue Valleywith them, where he becomes the shared pet of the Dugan-Whitmore's. He has a good judge of character and usually likes everyone, includingJustinandBeth. Buddy lived with his owner,Pat Dugan, and Pat's sonMike, in California. They moved around a lot in the past but seemingly settled in California after Pat metBarbara Whitmore. When Pat and Barbara married, Buddy gained new owners, Barbara andCourtney Whitmore. The new blended family moved from California toBlue Valley, Nebraska.

characters.name: Charles McNider
characters.description: Charles McNiderCharlesDr. Mid-NiteBiographical InformationFull NameCharles McNiderBorn1914[1](Age 117)GenderMaleAlter EgoDoctor Mid-Nite(Retired)Nickname(s)Chuck(ByBeth)McNider(ByRichard) andSylvester)Doc(BySylvester)StatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyRebecca McNider†(Daughter)Stanley McNider(Son)RomancesMyra McNider(Wife)FriendsPat DuganBeth ChapelRichard SwiftCourtney WhitmoreJames ChapelBridget ChapelEnemiesInjustice Society of AmericaThe Gambler†(Archenemy)EclipsoUltra-Humanite†Character InformationOccupationPhysicianLeader of theJustice Society of America(1941-2010)EducationMedical SchoolAffiliationJustice Society of AmericaSeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalHenry Thomas(Season 1)Alex Collins(Seasons2,3) Dr. Charles McNideris a supporting character inThe CWandDC UniverseseriesStargirl. He is a brilliant, introspective, and forward-thinking medical pioneer, and, better known by his alter-egoDoctor Mid-Nite, was a founding member of theJustice Society of America. With his trusty owlHootie, he acted as the team's resident doctor and detective. Born in the early 1900s, Charles' knowledge made him a valuable asset to the JSA until his alleged death in 2010 during a battle against the ISA. He was "defeated" byThe Shadewith his knowledge and mantle thought to be lost in the golden age of heroes.

characters.name: Christine Mahkent
characters.description: Christine MahkentBiographical InformationFull NameChristine MahkentBornNovember 1[1]Died2012(Mahkent Residence,Blue Valley)GenderFemaleStatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyCameron Mahkent(Son)Sofus Mahkent(Father-in-Law)Lily Mahkent†(Mother-in-Law)RomancesJordan Mahkent†(Husband)EnemiesBannerman ChemicalsCharacter InformationOccupationTeacherAffiliationInjustice Society of AmericaSeriesFirst Appearance"Icicle"Last Appearance"Icicle"PortrayalAmanda LavassaniChristine Mahkentwas a character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. Christine was the wife of the deceasedJordan Mahkentand the mother ofCameron. She was beloved but fell ill after being exposed to a toxin that gave her Cancer. On her death bed, she made Jordan promise to do whatever it takes to achieve his mission, and destroy anyone who stood in his way. Christine was in London when she first met Jordan, a businessman, who caught her attention. The feeling was mutual. He was nervous to ask her out as another man was pursuing her, who he implied to have killed. When he finally grew the courage to speak with her, she showed him the most beautiful drawings - of him. She had liked him too but was just waiting for him to make a move. They later became married and had a son together, Cameron. At some point, Jordan founded The American Dream to rebuild America one town at a time. At the same time, Jordan also founded the Injustice Society of America to thwart anyone who would attempt to get in their way. Christine found out about Jordan’s villainous actions and supported them, hoping to make a safer world for their son.

Part Two"PortrayalHenry Thomas Chuckwas a major character inThe CWandDC UniverseseriesStargirl. He was the artificial intelligence of Dr.Charles McNider, a brilliant, introspective, and forward-thinking medical pioneer. McNider was better known by his alter-ego Dr. Mid-Nite, and was a founding member of theJustice Society of America. Charles uploaded his artificial intelligence into hisgoggleswhich survived the 2010 battle that resulted in his believed physical death.

Cindy Burman is the most popular and most disliked student atBlue Valley High School. Cindy's mean girl antics initially placed her againstCourtney Whitmoreand her group of friends. After becoming friends with Courtney, she became a lot more nicer, however keeping some of her mean girl fierceness.

characters.name: Courtney Whitmore
characters.description: Courtney WhitmoreCourtneyStargirlBiographical InformationFull NameCourtney Elizabeth WhitmoreBorn2005GenderFemaleAlter EgoStarwomanStargirl(As a teenager)Nickname(s)Court(By family and friends)Blondie(ByYolanda)New Girl, Star-Spangled Bitch, Nitwit(ByCindy)Our Queen(ByJustin)Whitmore, Blonde Pinhead(ByArtemis)Wicked Girl, Whitmore Girl(Lily Mahkent)Star-Spangled Kid, Miss Whitmore(ByThe Shade)StatusAliveRelationshipsFamilyBarbara Whitmore(Mother)Sam Kurtis(Father; estranged)Pat Dugan(Stepfather)Mike Dugan(Stepbrother)RomancesCameron Mahkent(Ex-Boyfriend)[1]FriendsYolanda MontezBeth ChapelRick TylerHenry King Jr.†Richard SwiftJennie-Lynn HaydenCharles McNiderCindy BurmanTodd RiceJakeem WilliamsCameron MahkentEnemiesInjustice Society of America(Defeated)Eclipso(Defeated)Lily Mahkent†Ultra-Humanite†(Defeated)Character InformationOccupationLeader of theJustice Society of AmericaEducationBlue Valley High School(Graduated)Sherman High[2](Transferred)AffiliationJustice Society of AmericaSeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Chapter Thirteen: The Reckoning"PortrayalBrec BassingerMaizie Smith(Child) Courtney Elizabeth[3]Whitmoreis the main protagonist ofThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. Her smooth-going high school experience is derailed when hermothermarriesPat Duganand moves the household, which now includes Courtney's stepbrotherMike Duganand a familypet, from Los Angeles to bucolicBlue Valleyin distant Nebraska. Struggling to adapt as a Sophomore in anew school, Courtney discovers herstepfatherhas a secret past as a superhero sidekick. She also discovers an artifact of immense power, along-lost hero’scosmic staffthat has powers only she can wield. With the guidance of a reluctant Pat, Courtney becomes the superheroStargirland the unlikely inspiration for an entirely new generation of superheroes, and the leader of theJustice Society of America.

characters.name: Danny Matthews
characters.description: Danny MatthewsBiographical InformationFull NameDanny MatthewsGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsRomancesTodd Rice(Boyfriend)SeriesFirst Appearance"Chapter Seven: Infinity Inc. Part One"Last Appearance"Chapter Seven: Infinity Inc. Part One"PortrayalEddie FalshawDanny Matthewsis a minor character inThe CWseries,Stargirl. He is the boyfriend ofTodd Rice, whose birthday surprise goes awry. Six months ago inCivic City, Danny urgesToddto follow him. Todd questions where they're going. Danny asks if he trusts him, and Todd remarks that Danny knows he does. The boys arrive in a nice restaurant where a table is set with candles. Danny is making him dinner, though Todd is nervous about getting caught. Danny already disabled the alarm, having learned from the best. This kind of place is high stakes. They stay below the radar and they stay out of trouble, which Danny knows. He just wants to give him one special night where they aren't living like street rats. It's his birthday, and Danny knows this day's never easy for him. He gets his backpack, instructing Todd to sit down and open the bottle of wine while he gets started on the steaks. Todd sits down and finds an envelope addressed to him. Inside is a birthday card with two bus tickets. The card reads, "Todd - Happy birthday to you and your sister. We're going to find Jennie. I love you, - Danny". Todd smiles and removes a picture of himself and Jennie from his back pocket. He smiles at the picture until wind whooshes through the room, blowing out the candles in front of him.

characters.name: Denise Zarick
characters.description: Denise ZarickBiographical InformationFull NameDenise ZarickDiedAppx.October 29th, 2020(Blue Valley)GenderFemaleStatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyJoey Zarick†(Son)RomancesWilliam Zarick†(Husband)FriendsPat DuganSeriesFirst Appearance"S.T.R.I.P.E."Last Appearance"Wildcat""Summer School: Chapter Eleven"(Hallucination)PortrayalCynthia EvansDenise Zarickwas a minor character inThe CWandDC UniverseseriesStargirl. "Summer School: Chapter Eleven"(Hallucination) Denise was the doting mother ofJoey Zarickand the widow ofWilliam Zarick. Denise is a typical resident of Blue Valley - friendly and welcoming to new faces as well as a caring, loyal figure to her husband and a loving mother to her son. Curious with recent events and her husband's odd behavior, she gives an ominous vibe that she catches more than she lets on. Following Denise's son and husband's sudden deaths, she was distraught and inconsolable, eventually reaching a state of emotional numbness, and a frightful paranoia knowing of the fishy circumstances behind her husband's death that caused her to run. Even in her last moments, Denise would prove to be quite trusting in almost giving out critical information to a man that she barely met, if it meant coming clean of what she knew and if it could protect him and keep him on alert on what goes on in their town.

Part Two"Last Appearance"Summer School: Chapter Thirteen"PortrayalNick TarabayMilo Stein(PossessingBruce)Meg DeLacy(PossessingCindy)Brec Bassinger(PossessingCourtney) Eclipsois a major antagonist in theThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. He is a demonic entity of corruption and vengeance, trapped inside ablack diamond. Brimming with a cold, terrifying darkness, he exploits the flaws of others, reveling in the impure and sinful, sadistically feeding off the dark side of humanity. Years after his defeat by the Golden AgeJustice Society of America, Eclipso formed an alliance withCindy Burmanto take down the new JSA.

characters.name: Emily Swift
characters.description: Emily SwiftBiographical InformationFull NameEmily SwiftBornCirca. 1800sGenderFemaleStatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyMr. Swift(Father)Richard Swift(Brother)SeriesFirst Appearance"Chapter Eight: Infinity Inc. Part Two"(Construct)Last Appearance"Chapter Eight: Infinity Inc. Part Two"(Construct)PortrayalArianne MartinEmily Swiftis a minor character inThe CWseries,Stargirl. She is the late sister ofRichard Swift, having died of an illness early on in the man's immortality. Her death shaped the way Richard viewed others and began his reluctance to form attachments. Emily was born in London, England in the 1800s to an unnamed father. She was the sister ofRichard Swift, a con man who gained unspeakable power in the mid-1800s. He tried not to abuse his power when in the presence of his sister. When their father fell ill, Richard left him in Emily's care, and wasn't there when he died. Emily soon fell ill as well, and died without her brother by her side.

characters.name: Gar Logan
characters.description: Gar LoganBiographical InformationFull NameGar LoganGenderMaleAlter EgoBeast BoyStatusAliveCharacter InformationOccupationMember of the TitansAffiliationTitansSeriesFirst Appearance"Dude, Where's My Gar?"Last Appearance"Dude, Where's My Gar?"PortrayalRyan PotterGar Loganis a main character in theHBO Maxseries,DC's Titans. His character was part of a brief crossover betweenStargirlandTitans. Gar is funny, charming, and a member of the Titans through his meta-human alter-ego,Beast Boy. He gets lost in the multiverse and ends up inBlue Valley, Nebraska, where he encounters ayoung hero. Gar falls through the Multiverse and lands hard on the pavement outsideBlue Valley High School. He stands, groaning in pain. A pair ofblue bootslands on the ground andStargirlcautiously approaches the stranger while holding herCosmic Staff. She calls out to him, and he startles, staring at the staff oddly. She asks if he’s alright, and he whispers back to ask where he is. She explains he's inBlue Valley, Nebraska, which depletes him until he realizes he’s in North America. She looks up to the sky as she says he came from nowhere, just a light in the sky that she saw. The staff warbles and jerks toward Gar, who flinches away. Stargirl pulls it back and reprimands Cosmo, chuckling out an apology. She asks if he needs help which makes him laugh. Suddenly serious, he says he needs to get back to his family. She knows how importantfamilyis, and he gets the sense that she does. He needs to get to them but he’s not sure where that is. He turns away from her and positions himself, commanding to be taken to his family, but nothing happens. She whispers to ask him who he’s talking to. He takes a deep breath then shouts to be taken and a bright red light swirls around him. It sucks him out of Blue Valley.[1]

He takes a deep breath then shouts to be taken and a bright red light swirls around him. It sucks him out of Blue Valley.[1]

characters.name: Harold Sherman
characters.description: Harold ShermanBiographical InformationFull NameHarold ShermanGenderMaleStatusAliveRelationshipsFriendsPaul DeisingerCharacter InformationOccupationPrincipal atBVHSAffiliationBlue Valley High SchoolSeriesFirst Appearance"Summer School: Chapter One"Last Appearance"Summer School: Chapter Seven"PortrayalTywayne WheattHarold Shermanis a minor character inThe CWseries,Stargirl. He is the new principal atBlue Valley High School, who assigned Courtney with summer school. On the last day of school,Courtney WhitmoreattacksArtemis Crockwith her own hockey stick, mistaking her passing by as aggression. Principal Sherman escorts Courtney to his office where she's reprimanded.BarbaraandPatarrive, with her asking what Courtney did. It's more about what shedidn'tdo. Sherman knows from personal experience that moving in the middle of the high school isn't easy, but the good news, is Courtney's managed to acclimate. She's made friends even if they are questionable choices in that department, but she seems to have found her place at Blue Valley High. Just as he has, in the past few months. He mentions the incident today with Artemis Crock, which is news to the Whitmore-Dugan's.

characters.name: Henry King Jr.
characters.description: Henry King Jr.Biographical InformationFull NameHenry King Jr.Born2004DiedNovember 5th, 2020(Aged 16)(Tunnels,Blue Valley, Nebraska)GenderMaleAlter EgoBrainwave Jr.Nickname(s)Junior(ByDr. KingandRick)StatusDeceasedRelationshipsFamilyHenry King Sr.†(Father)Merry King†(Mother)Sylvester Pemberton(Maternal Uncle)Unnamed Paternal GrandfatherUnnamed Maternal Grandparents †RomancesCindy Burman(Ex-Girlfriend)Yolanda Montez(Ex-Girlfriend)FriendsBrian Tanner BalloidTravis ThomasCourtney WhitmoreEnemiesHenry King Sr.†Jordan Mahkent†Shiro Ito†Character InformationOccupationStudent(Formerly)EducationBlue Valley High SchoolAffiliationJustice Society of America(Alliance)SeriesFirst Appearance"Stargirl"Last Appearance"Brainwave Jr."(Physical)"Summer School: Chapter Seven"(Hallucination)PortrayalJake Austin Walker Henry King Jr.was a major character inThe CWandDC Universeseries,Stargirl. He was a student atBlue Valley High Schooland a skilled football player, whose elite popularity gave him a cocky edge. Henry was known as an arrogant, spoiled, rich teenager with little respect or care for others. His disrespect forYolandacaused tension between him and her newfriend group, which escalated when Henry exhibited his father's telekinetic abilities. He was alone in his discovery of his powers, but learned to accept help fromCourtney Whitmore. The alliance began a complicated battle for Henry betweengoodandevil.
